home.question: How much does windshield replacement cost in Victorville, CA?
home.answer: Aftermarket windshield replacement in Victorville typically costs $250–$500. OEM glass runs $500–$1,500+. Most comprehensive insurance covers replacement with minimal deductible. Exact pricing depends on your vehicle make/model and glass type. Call for a free quote.

home.question: Is it illegal to drive with a cracked windshield in California?
home.answer: Yes. California Vehicle Code 26708 prohibits driving with damage in your driver's line of sight. Even small cracks can result in citations. Damage outside the vision area may be repairable. Don't wait—repair or replace immediately for safety and legal compliance.

home.question: Can a 2-inch crack in a windshield be repaired?
home.answer: It depends on location. California repair standards allow cracks up to 12 inches if outside the driver's line of sight. A 2-inch crack is usually repairable if it's not in your direct view. Bring it in for inspection—repairs cost $100–$400 and save money versus replacement.

home.question: Is it worth using insurance for a cracked windshield?
home.answer: Usually yes. Most comprehensive policies cover windshield claims with $0–$500 deductible. Since repairs cost $100–$400, filing a claim often makes sense. Glass claims rarely raise premiums. Check your policy details or call your insurer before scheduling service.

home.question: Who is responsible if a rock hits my windshield?
home.answer: Generally, you're responsible unless another vehicle kicked up the debris. Hit-and-run victims may use their own comprehensive coverage. Liability typically doesn't cover windshield damage unless the other driver is identified. File a claim or pay out-of-pocket—most repairs are quick and affordable.
